title: T-015 — RLS contract test: withOrgContext via app.set_org_context
prompt:
# T-015 — RLS contract: app.current_org_id() enforcement via foundation setter

## Goal
Add a contract test confirming RLS denies cross-org SELECT on every settings table. Add helper `withOrgContext(sessionToken, orgId, fn)` for Server Actions that opens a transaction and calls the non-spoofable foundation setter `SELECT app.set_org_context($1::uuid, $2::uuid)` (see `packages/db/migrations/002-rls-baseline.sql`). The setter writes into `app.active_org_contexts` keyed by backend_pid + transaction_id, so RLS policies that read via `app.current_org_id()` resolve correctly for the duration of the tx; the row is naturally invalidated on commit/rollback (next txid).

## Files in scope
- apps/web/lib/db/with-org-context.ts [create]
- apps/web/lib/db/with-org-context.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria (all must hold)
- Given two orgs A and B exist, when a user authenticated as A reads users for B inside `withOrgContext(sessionToken_A, A.id, fn)`, then the query returns 0 rows (RLS denies).
- Given the helper `withOrgContext(sessionToken, orgId, fn)` is invoked, when fn issues SQL, then `app.current_org_id()` returns `orgId` only for the duration of the transaction.
- Given the contract test runs across all 12 RLS-scoped tables, when executed, then each table denies cross-org access (12 assertions pass).
- Given the helper source is inspected, when grep'd, then it contains NO `current_setting('app.tenant_id'|'app.current_org_id')` and NO `SET LOCAL app.current_org_id` — it only calls `app.set_org_context(...)`.

## Test strategy (RED first)
1. Add a failing test that exercises the acceptance criteria above. The RED command is:
   `pnpm --filter @monopilot/web vitest run apps/web/lib/db/with-org-context.test.ts`
2. Run RED — it must fail before any production code is written.
3. Implement the smallest change needed for GREEN.
4. Re-run RED command — must pass.
5. Run `pnpm typecheck` and `pnpm lint` in the changed package; both must pass.

## Risk red lines
- Do not read `current_setting('app.tenant_id')` or `current_setting('app.current_org_id')` directly — use the foundation `app.current_org_id()` function so the NULL-safe setter contract is preserved.
- Do not use `SET LOCAL app.current_org_id` (spoofable GUC) — call `app.set_org_context(session_token, org_id)` instead.
- Do not edit files outside the listed scope without documenting why in CLOSEOUT.
- Do not weaken existing acceptance criteria from prior tasks.
- Do not introduce REST endpoints; use Server Actions per stack contract.
- Do not commit secrets or vault keys.

## Closeout evidence required
- changed_files (with line counts)
- exact test commands and outputs (RED→GREEN, typecheck, lint)
- acceptance_criteria_status (one row per AC: pass/fail)
- deviations_from_prd (or "none")
- git_status

Stack: TS + Next.js App Router + Drizzle + pnpm + vitest + Playwright + Server Actions. Primitives in `packages/ui`. PRD: `docs/prd/02-SETTINGS-PRD.md` v3.5.

pipeline_inputs.description: RLS helper + cross-org contract test using foundation app.set_org_context() setter (non-spoofable).
pipeline_inputs.details: Helper opens transaction, runs `SELECT app.set_org_context($1::uuid, $2::uuid)` (session_token, org_id), calls fn, commits or rolls back. Reject calls without sessionToken/orgId. Provide typed return. RLS uses foundation `app.current_org_id()` function (Wave0 decision; see `_meta/audits/2026-05-14-architecture-and-cross-cutting-gaps.md` §Tenant-context).
acceptance_criteria: Cross-org SELECT returns 0 rows on every settings table when run inside withOrgContext(sessionToken_A, A.id, fn).
Helper source contains no current_setting('app.tenant_id'|'app.current_org_id') GUC reads and no SET LOCAL app.current_org_id — only app.set_org_context(...).
test_strategy: RED: write contract test for 12 tables using app_user connection + app.set_org_context().
GREEN: implement helper + ensure RLS policies allow scope.
risk_red_lines: Do not read current_setting('app.tenant_id') or current_setting('app.current_org_id') directly — use the foundation app.current_org_id() function so the NULL-safe setter contract is preserved.
Do not use SET LOCAL app.current_org_id (spoofable GUC) — call app.set_org_context(session_token, org_id) instead.
Do not bypass RLS by using a service role connection from app code.
